formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes in this Humble, TX suburban neighborhood feature predominantly hip-style roofs with moderate visual characteristics consistent with a 6:12 pitch. Ground shadows cast from the eave edges extend roughly 1–1.5× the overhang width, indicating a moderate but not steep slope. The hip roof planes are clearly visible from the near-nadir view but are not dramatically foreshortened (which would indicate steeper pitch) nor nearly flat-looking (which would indicate shallower pitch). The ridge is noticeably inset from the eave perimeter but occupies a moderate proportion of the footprint. Regionally, Houston-area suburban construction from the 2000s–2010s strongly favors 6:12 as the standard design pitch, and all visual cues here align with that estimate.
footprint_rationale: Target identified as the large dark charcoal/gray hip-roofed house at the bottom of the Kenswick Meadows Ct cul-de-sac (lower-center of image). The house is L-shaped: a main rectangular section (~73 px E-W × ~55 px N-S = 61.9 ft × 46.7 ft) plus an attached garage/wing (~30 px × ~35 px = 25.4 ft × 29.7 ft). Net footprint after subtracting the shared-wall overlap and adjusting for roof outline vs. wall footprint discrepancy ≈ 3,100 sq ft. Roof type appears to be an intersecting hip with no visible gable ends (no rakes), yielding a main ridge of ~30 ft, ~130 ft of hip lines (diagonal corner edges from ridge to eave corners), ~20 ft of valley where the wing intersects the main body, and ~155 ft of eave running along the full L-shaped lower perimeter. Confidence is moderate-low due to image resolution limitations and uncertainty in precisely identifying this parcel vs. adjacent homes on the cul-de-sac.
